      -- Use the optional sampling theorem to relate the stopped values at different times

        -- Apply the optional sampling theorem for the submartingale

    exact h2
  exact h_main

    filter_upwards [h₂] with ω hω
    exact hω
  exact h₁

But to get from `E[X τ | 𝓕 (τ ⊓ σ)]` to `E[X τ | 𝓕 σ]`, we need to use that `E[X τ | 𝓕 σ] = E[E[X τ | 𝓕 (τ ⊓ σ)] | 𝓕 σ]`.

But this would imply that `E[X τ | 𝓕 σ] ≥ E[X τ | 𝓕 (τ ⊓ σ)]` if `E[X τ | 𝓕 (τ ⊓ σ)]` is non-negative, but we don't have that.

have h₀ : ∀ ω, τ ω ≤ k → stoppedValue X (τ ⊓ σ) ω ≤ P[stoppedValue X τ | hσ.measurableSpace] ω := by
    intro ω hω
    have h₁ : (τ ⊓ σ) ω ≤ τ ω := by
      simp [inf_le_left]
    have h₂ : stoppedValue X (τ ⊓ σ) ω ≤ stoppedValue X τ ω := by
      have h₃ : (τ ⊓ σ) ω ≤ τ ω := h₁
      have h₄ : stoppedValue X (τ ⊓ σ) ω = X ((τ ⊓ σ) ω) ω := rfl
      have h₅ : stoppedValue X τ ω = X (τ ω) ω := rfl
      rw [h₄, h₅]
      have h₆ : (τ ⊓ σ) ω ≤ τ ω := h₃
      have h₇ : IsClosedOrderUpperSet E := by
        exact inferInstance
      have h₈ : (fun i : ι => X i ω) ( (τ ⊓ σ) ω ) ≤ (fun i : ι => X i ω) (τ ω) := by
        have h₉ : ContinuousOn (fun i : ι => X i ω) (Set.Iic (τ ω)) := by
          have h₁₀ : RightContinuous (fun i : ι => X i ω) := hX2 ω
          have h₁₁ : ContinuousOn (fun i : ι => X i ω) (Set.Iic (τ ω)) := by
            apply ContinuousWithinAt.continuousOn
            intro i hi
            have h₁₂ : i ≤ τ ω := hi
            exact h₁₀.continuousWithinAt (by simp_all [hi])
          exact h₁₁
        have h₁₃ : (τ ⊓ σ) ω ∈ Set.Iic (τ ω) := by
          simp_all [inf_le_left]
        have h₁₄ : (fun i : ι => X i ω) ((τ ⊓ σ) ω) ≤ (fun i : ι => X i ω) (τ ω) := by
          have h₁₅ : MonotoneOn (fun i : ι => X i ω) (Set.Iic (τ ω)) := by
            intro i hi j hj hij
            have h₁₆ : i ≤ j := hij
            have h₁₇ : j ≤ τ ω := by
              simp_all [Set.mem_Iic]
              <;> aesop
            have h₁₈ : i ≤ τ ω := by
              simp_all [Set.mem_Iic]
              <;> aesop
            have h₁₉ : i ∈ Set.Iic (τ ω) := by
              simp_all [Set.mem_Iic]
              <;> aesop
            have h₂₀ : j ∈ Set.Iic (τ ω) := by
              simp_all [Set.mem_Iic]
              <;> aesop
            have h₂₁ : X i ω ≤ X j ω := by
              have h₂₂ : Submartingale X 𝓕 P := hX1
              exact h₂₂.monotonicity (by simp_all [Set.mem_Iic]) (by simp_all [Set.mem_Iic]) h₁₆
            exact h₂₁
          have h₂₃ : (τ ⊓ σ) ω ≤ τ ω := h₁
          have h₂₄ : (τ ⊓ σ) ω ∈ Set.Iic (τ ω) := by
            simp_all [Set.mem_Iic]
            <;> aesop
          have h₂₅ : τ ω ∈ Set.Iic (τ ω) := by
            simp_all [Set.mem_Iic]
            <;> aesop
          have h₂₆ : (τ ⊓ σ) ω ≤ τ ω := h₁
          exact h₁₅ h₂₄ h₂₅ h₂₆
        exact h₁₄
      simpa [h₄, h₅] using h₈
    have h₃ : stoppedValue X τ ω ≤ P[stoppedValue X τ | hσ.measurableSpace] ω := by
      have h₄ : AEStopStronglyMeasurable P ( stoppedValue X τ ) := by
        have h₅ : hσ.IsLUB := hσ.isLUB
        have h₆ : hσ.IsLUB.isApproximating := h₅.isApproximating
        sorry
      have h₇ : ∀ ω, stoppedValue X τ ω ≤ P[stoppedValue X τ | hσ.measurableSpace] ω := by
        sorry
      exact h₇ ω
    exact le_trans h₂ h₃
  have h₁ : stoppedValue X (τ ⊓ σ) ≤ᵐ[P] P[stoppedValue X τ|hσ.measurableSpace] := by
    filter_upwards [hτk] with ω hω
    exact h₀ ω hω
